name: Structured logs with correlation IDs
description: Machine-readable logs tied to request or job execution
when_to_use: Every production backend and worker
trade_offs: Requires consistent logging fields and PII discipline
implementation_notes: Include request_id user_id tenant_id operation duration status and error class
keywords: structured logs correlation id request id

name: Distributed tracing
description: Trace requests across services databases queues and external calls
when_to_use: Microservices complex monoliths queues and slow request debugging
implementation_notes: Propagate trace context across HTTP and queue messages

name: RED metrics
description: Track rate errors and duration for services and endpoints
when_to_use: APIs workers and critical dependencies
trade_offs: Metrics cardinality can explode if labels are unbounded
implementation_notes: Use route templates not raw URLs and define p50 p95 p99 latency
keywords: metrics rate errors duration p95 p99

name: SLO with error budget
description: Define reliability target and alert on user-impacting burn rate
when_to_use: Production systems with uptime or latency commitments
trade_offs: Requires product agreement on reliability targets
implementation_notes: Create SLI for availability latency correctness and alert on budget burn

name: Audit log
description: Append-only record of security or business-critical actions
when_to_use: Admin actions account changes financial records regulated systems
trade_offs: Can expose sensitive data if not designed carefully
implementation_notes: Store actor action target timestamp metadata and reason without secrets

name: Health and readiness checks
description: Expose process liveness and dependency readiness
when_to_use: Containers load balancers orchestrators and deploy pipelines
trade_offs: Deep checks can cause cascading load
implementation_notes: Keep liveness shallow and readiness dependency-aware

name: Error tracking
description: Group exceptions and regressions across releases
when_to_use: APIs workers and frontend-facing backend errors
trade_offs: Can collect sensitive data if not filtered
implementation_notes: Sanitize payloads and tag release environment tenant and route

name: Continuous profiling
description: Find CPU memory and allocation hotspots in production
when_to_use: High-throughput services and performance investigations
trade_offs: Adds overhead and requires sampling control
implementation_notes: Use low-overhead profiling and correlate with deploys

name: Business metrics
description: Track domain outcomes beyond technical health
when_to_use: Payments subscriptions orders onboarding and conversion
implementation_notes: Emit counters and gauges for critical domain events

name: PII-safe logging
description: Prevent secrets and personal data from entering logs
when_to_use: Regulated apps auth payment healthcare and support systems
implementation_notes: Redact tokens passwords emails where possible and denylist sensitive fields

name: Queue observability
description: Monitor queue depth lag age retries and DLQ count
when_to_use: Any worker based system
trade_offs: Metrics need per queue ownership and alert thresholds
implementation_notes: Alert on oldest message age not just queue depth
keywords: queue depth lag dlq worker metrics

name: Deployment markers
description: Correlate metrics traces and errors with releases
when_to_use: Any production system with frequent deploys
implementation_notes: Send release version commit sha and deploy timestamp to observability tools
keywords: deploy marker release version commit observability

name: Correlation ID propagation
description: Pass request ID through HTTP queue and worker boundaries
when_to_use: Distributed systems and async workflows
implementation_notes: Generate at edge and propagate in every log and span

name: Log sampling
description: Reduce high-volume logs while preserving signal
when_to_use: High-throughput APIs and noisy success logs
implementation_notes: Sample normal logs but keep errors and security events

name: Structured domain events
description: Log domain events with stable names and fields
when_to_use: Auditing analytics and debugging business flows
implementation_notes: Use consistent event_name actor target tenant and result fields

name: USE metrics
description: Track utilization saturation and errors for resources
when_to_use: Databases queues CPU memory and connection pools
implementation_notes: Measure utilization saturation errors per shared resource

name: Golden signals
description: Track latency traffic errors and saturation
when_to_use: User-facing services and APIs
implementation_notes: Create dashboard per service with p50 p95 p99 and error rate

name: Dependency metrics
description: Measure each downstream call separately
when_to_use: APIs with databases caches and external services
trade_offs: High cardinality if labels include raw URL
implementation_notes: Label by dependency operation and status class

name: Trace sampling policy
description: Control trace volume while keeping useful failures
when_to_use: High traffic services using distributed tracing
trade_offs: Too much sampling loses rare issues
implementation_notes: Keep all errors and sample normal traffic by rate

name: Async trace context
description: Propagate trace context through queue messages
when_to_use: Workers event consumers and delayed jobs
implementation_notes: Inject traceparent into message and continue span in worker

name: Burn rate alert
description: Alert on fast and slow SLO budget burn
when_to_use: Production services with defined SLOs
implementation_notes: Use multi-window burn rate alerts

name: Symptom-based alerting
description: Alert on user impact rather than every cause
when_to_use: Production operations and on-call
implementation_notes: Alert on failed requests latency and data loss symptoms

name: Runbook link in alert
description: Attach response steps to actionable alerts
when_to_use: On-call systems and recurring incidents
implementation_notes: Include dashboard links owner and first mitigation steps

name: Data access audit
description: Record reads of sensitive records
when_to_use: Healthcare finance admin and privacy-sensitive systems
implementation_notes: Audit actor target reason and access path for sensitive reads

name: Synthetic transaction monitor
description: Continuously test critical user journeys from outside
when_to_use: Login checkout webhook and public APIs
trade_offs: Can create noise if test data breaks
implementation_notes: Run synthetic checks with dedicated test accounts
